testimonial1.quote: The system has been running flawlessly since day one. In just 8 months, we've already saved over 4,000 BGN. Solaron was exceptionally professional — from the first meeting to the final test.
testimonial1.name: Ivan Petrov

testimonial2.quote: Professional approach from start to finish. The installation was completed in 2 days, and the system is performing above expectations. I've already recommended Solaron to three neighbors.
testimonial2.name: Maria Georgieva

testimonial3.quote: The investment paid for itself in less than 4 years. Now we practically have free electricity. The quality of the components and workmanship is world-class.

testimonial4.quote: Solaron designed and installed the system with incredible precision. The monitoring platform gives us full visibility of production in real time.

environmentalImpact.title: Our Contribution to the Planet
environmentalImpact.subtitle: Real impact, measured from our installations across Bulgaria.
environmentalImpact.calculationExplanation: Calculations are based on real data from our installations in Bulgaria. Average CO₂ savings are calculated using the Bulgarian electricity grid emission factor (0.47 kg CO₂/kWh). The tree equivalent uses the EPA standard — one tree absorbs ~22 kg CO₂ per year. Powered households are calculated based on average consumption of 3,500 kWh/yr for a Bulgarian household.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about solar systems and the installation process.
faq.notFoundTitle: Can't find the answer to your question?
faq.notFoundSubtitle: Contact us for a free consultation.
faq.q1: How much does a solar system installation cost?
faq.a1: The price depends on the capacity and system type. For residential systems from 3 to 10 kWp, prices range between 5,000 and 18,000 BGN. We offer a free consultation and personalized quote.
faq.q2: How long does it take for the investment to pay off?
faq.a2: At current electricity prices, a typical residential system pays for itself in 3–5 years. With net metering and optimal orientation, this period can be even shorter.
faq.a3: Net metering is a mechanism where excess produced energy is fed back into the grid and deducted from your bill. In Bulgaria, households can use net metering for systems up to 30 kWp.
faq.q4: How long does the installation take?
faq.a4: A typical residential system installation takes 1–3 days. For larger commercial systems — 1–2 weeks. The entire process from consultation to commissioning takes 2–4 weeks.
faq.a5: We offer a 30-year warranty on solar panels, 25-year on inverters, and 10-year on batteries. Additionally, we provide a 5-year installation warranty.
faq.q6: Do I need a building permit?
faq.a6: For systems up to 30 kWp, a building permit is generally not required. For larger systems, project documentation is needed. We handle all the paperwork.

ctaSplit.title: Ready for solar energy?
ctaSplit.subtitle: Contact us for a free consultation and personalized quote.

solarPanels.title: Solar Panels
solarPanels.desc1: Our panels use Metal Wrap Through (MWT) technology — an innovative approach where conductors pass through the back side of the cell, eliminating shading from traditional busbar strips.
solarPanels.desc2: The result is a larger active surface area, higher efficiency, and significantly longer service life thanks to reduced mechanical stress.
solarPanels.pullQuote: MWT technology eliminates microcracks and increases efficiency by up to 3%

batteries.title: Batteries
batteries.desc: Lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries offer unmatched safety and long life. Store excess energy from the day and use it in the evening — achieve complete energy independence without compromise.

inverters.title: Inverters
inverters.desc1: SolarEdge inverters with HD-Wave technology convert DC power from panels into AC power with a record-breaking 99.5% efficiency. Each panel is individually optimized through P950 optimizers.
inverters.desc2: The built-in monitoring platform gives you full visibility of production in real time — from each individual panel to the entire system.

shared.ctaSubtitle: Contact us for a free consultation and customized quote.

overview.title: Solutions | Solaron
overview.description: Solar solutions for home, business, industry, and agriculture. Off-grid systems and solar carports.

zaDoma.title: Solar System for Home | Solaron
zaDoma.description: Save up to 80% on your electricity bill. Increase your property value. Free consultation.
zaDoma.heroSubtitle: Save up to 80% on your electricity bill. Increase your property value. Independence from the grid.
zaDoma.benefit1Desc: Generate your own energy and pay minimal electricity bills. The system pays for itself in 4–6 years.
zaDoma.benefit2Desc: Less dependence on fluctuating electricity prices. With a battery, you have power even during grid outages.
zaDoma.benefit2Feature2Desc: Sell excess energy back to the grid
zaDoma.benefit3Desc: Equivalent to 200 trees planted per year.
zaDoma.benefit4Desc: Properties with solar systems sell faster and at a higher price.
zaDoma.benefit5Desc: Residential solar systems are exempt from VAT — a direct discount on the price.
zaDoma.beforeAfterNote: Example for a household with a 5 kWp system at 250 BGN/mo. bill
zaDoma.processStep1Desc: We analyze your roof, consumption, and goals. We recommend the optimal system.
zaDoma.processStep2Desc: We prepare the technical design, submit documents, and coordinate with the utility company.
zaDoma.processStep3Desc: Professional installation by a certified team. We test every component.
zaDoma.processStep4Desc: The system produces energy from day one. Monitor everything in real time.
zaDoma.calculatorSubtitle: Enter your property details and see a personalized projection
zaDoma.financingSubtitle: Three ways to finance your system — choose the one that works for you
zaDoma.financingCashDesc: One-time payment with maximum discount. Lowest total price.
zaDoma.financingCreditDesc: Bank loan with low interest. Monthly payment is less than the savings.
zaDoma.financingCreditNote: Monthly payment is comparable to or less than your current electricity bill
zaDoma.financingLeasingDesc: Operating lease — no down payment. Pay less than your current bill.
zaDoma.componentsSubtitle: We work with three inverter brands and Tier-1+ panels — from premium to optimal price-to-quality ratio
zaDoma.warrantySubtitle: Every system is protected by a triple warranty for your peace of mind
zaDoma.faq1Q: Is a solar system suitable for my roof?
zaDoma.faq1A: Most roofs with solid construction and south or west-facing exposure are suitable. We conduct a free survey and shade analysis before proposing a solution.
zaDoma.faq2Q: How much space do the panels take up?
zaDoma.faq2A: A 5 kWp system requires about 25–30 m² of unshaded area. The exact number of panels is determined after a roof survey.
zaDoma.faq3Q: What happens at night or on cloudy days?
zaDoma.faq3A: Without a battery, you use grid power. On cloudy days, production is lower but doesn't stop. With a battery, you store energy for the evening and night.
zaDoma.faq4Q: How long does the installation take?
zaDoma.faq4A: For a typical residential system — 1 to 3 working days. Documentation and coordination with the utility company take an additional 2–4 weeks.
zaDoma.faq5Q: Are there subsidies for residential solar systems?
zaDoma.faq5A: Currently, there are no direct subsidies for households in Bulgaria, but the zero VAT rate (0% instead of 20%) significantly reduces the investment.
zaDoma.faq6A: Panels have a 25-year performance warranty. Inverters — 12 years with an extension option. Installation — 5 years by Solaron.
zaDoma.faq7A: Net metering allows you to sell excess energy back to the grid. You only pay the difference between produced and consumed energy over a 12-month period.
zaDoma.faq8A: For systems up to 30 kWp for self-consumption, no building permit is required. Only a notification to the utility company and a connection agreement are needed, which we arrange.

zaBiznesa.title: Solar System for Business | Solaron
zaBiznesa.description: Solar system for business and commercial PV systems — energy audit, design, and installation.
zaBiznesa.heroSubtitle: Reduce operating costs by up to 70%. Accelerated tax depreciation. Professional energy audit.

zaIndustriyata.title: Industrial Solar System | Solaron
zaIndustriyata.description: Large-scale industrial photovoltaic systems — from 100 kWp to megawatt power plants.
zaIndustriyata.heroSubtitle: From 100 kWp to megawatt power plants — we design, build, and optimize industrial photovoltaic systems with guaranteed returns.
zaIndustriyata.challengeDesc1: Industrial enterprises in Bulgaria pay an average of 0.22–0.28 BGN/kWh, with the peak component reaching up to 40% of the bill.
zaIndustriyata.challengeDesc2: A solar system not only reduces the cost per kWh but also 'shaves' peak consumption — exactly when the sun is strongest, your production is running at full capacity.
zaIndustriyata.archSubtitle: Every industrial system is individually designed for maximum efficiency and integration with your processes.
zaIndustriyata.benefit1Desc: Reduce peak power by up to 50%. Solar production covers exactly the peak hours of consumption.
zaIndustriyata.benefit2Desc: With battery storage and intelligent load management, your production continues even during outages.
zaIndustriyata.benefit3Desc: ESG reporting, carbon credits, and compliance with the European Sustainability Directive — be prepared for CSRD.
zaIndustriyata.benefit4Desc: Modular architecture allows phased expansion — start with the roof and add a ground-mounted system as the business grows.
zaIndustriyata.auditStep1Desc: Detailed review of 12-month load profiles, peak hours, and tariff structures.
zaIndustriyata.auditStep2Desc: Measurement of roof areas, inclinations, shading, and grid access.
zaIndustriyata.auditStep3Desc: PVsyst production modeling, financial model with NPV/IRR, and optimal configuration.
zaIndustriyata.auditStep4Desc: Professional installation without disrupting production. Testing, grid connection, and 24/7 monitoring.
zaIndustriyata.videoSubtitle: Our team on an industrial roof — from panel deployment to the finished structure.

zaZemedelieto.title: Solar System for Agriculture | Solaron
zaZemedelieto.description: Agrivoltaics, irrigation, and energy independence for farms.
zaZemedelieto.heroSubtitle: Dual land use — harvest and clean energy. Reduce costs for irrigation, storage, and processing.
zaZemedelieto.cropBenefitsDesc: Elevated solar panels provide partial shade, reduce evaporation, and protect against hail.
cropBenefits: Better yields for lettuce, herbs, strawberries
zaZemedelieto.farmBenefitsDesc: Additional income from selling electricity or full coverage of your own energy needs.
farmBenefits: Free energy for pumps and systems
zaZemedelieto.irrigationDesc: A typical irrigation pump consumes 5–15 kW. A 10 kWp solar system fully covers the needs and operates exactly when water is needed most — during summer.
zaZemedelieto.seasonalSubtitle: Solar energy aligns perfectly with agricultural needs
zaZemedelieto.ctaSubtitle: Free consultation for agricultural solar solutions — from irrigation to energy independence.

solarenKarport.title: Solar Carport | Solaron
solarenKarport.description: Solar carport — parking and clean energy. EV charging, dual revenue.
solarenKarport.heroSubtitle: Dual revenue — from parking and from clean energy.
solarenKarport.dualDesc: A solar carport turns every square meter of parking into a revenue source. The structure protects vehicles from the elements, while rooftop panels produce clean electricity.
solarenKarport.dualDesc2: With integrated EV charging stations, the carport becomes a full-fledged energy hub — parking, charging, and production in one place.
solarenKarport.techSubtitle: Every component is designed for maximum efficiency and durability.
solarenKarport.tech1Desc: IP65 protection, 25+ year lifespan. No corrosion, no maintenance.
solarenKarport.tech2Desc: Up to 25% higher yield thanks to double-sided light absorption.
solarenKarport.tech3Desc: AC/DC charging stations integrated into the structure from the design phase.
solarenKarport.tech4Desc: Real-time tracking of production, consumption, and EV charging revenue.
solarenKarport.benefitsSubtitle: Four reasons to choose a solar carport for your business.
solarenKarport.benefit1Desc: The aluminum structure protects vehicles from sun, hail, and rain — year-round.
solarenKarport.benefit2Desc: Bifacial panels produce up to 25% more energy than standard panels.
solarenKarport.benefit3Desc: Integrated charging stations for electric vehicles — powered directly by the carport.
solarenKarport.benefit4Desc: Dual revenue: parking fees and selling excess energy to the grid.
solarenKarport.evSubtitle: Integrate charging stations directly into the carport structure. Energy comes from the rooftop panels.
solarenKarport.revenueModelSubtitle: The combination of parking and clean energy creates a sustainable business model with accelerated returns.

avtonomniSistemi.title: Off-Grid Systems | Solaron
avtonomniSistemi.description: Off-grid solar systems for complete energy independence. LiFePO₄ batteries, 99.9% uptime.
avtonomniSistemi.whyDesc1: In remote areas, the power grid is unreliable or completely absent. Outages are frequent, and connecting is expensive and slow. Off-grid systems give you full control over your energy.
avtonomniSistemi.whyDesc2: With modern LiFePO₄ batteries and high-efficiency panels, off-grid systems now provide comfort equal to grid-connected — without monthly bills and without dependence on a provider.
avtonomniSistemi.archSubtitle: Four key components designed to work seamlessly together
avtonomniSistemi.comp1Desc: Convert sunlight into direct current. The primary energy source.
avtonomniSistemi.comp2Desc: Regulates charging from the panels. Extracts maximum power under any conditions.
avtonomniSistemi.comp3Desc: Stores energy for nighttime and cloudy days. The most reliable battery technology.
avtonomniSistemi.comp4Desc: Converts DC to AC and manages the entire energy flow. Operates even at zero grid.
avtonomniSistemi.benefit1Desc: Zero dependence on the grid and price fluctuations.
avtonomniSistemi.benefit2Desc: 99.9% uptime with automatic switchover. No interruptions.
avtonomniSistemi.benefit3Desc: Ideal solution for mountain houses, farms, and sites without grid access.
avtonomniSistemi.benefit4Desc: 3–7 days of autonomy without sun. LiFePO₄ batteries with 6000+ cycles and 15+ year lifespan.
avtonomniSistemi.useCasesSubtitle: Off-grid systems are ideal wherever the grid is unreliable or unavailable
avtonomniSistemi.useCase1Desc: Full autonomy for vacation or permanent mountain property.
avtonomniSistemi.useCase2Desc: Uninterrupted power for telecom towers, repeaters, and remote stations.
avtonomniSistemi.useCase3Desc: Powering irrigation pumps, cooling systems, and electric fences.
avtonomniSistemi.autonomyTitle: How many days of autonomy do you have?
avtonomniSistemi.autonomySubtitle: Duration depends on battery array size and daily consumption
avtonomniSistemi.autonomyChartLabel: Days of autonomy at average consumption of 7 kWh/day

shared.ctaSubtitle: Configure your system online and receive a personalized quote in minutes.

solarniPaneli.title: Solar Panels | Solaron
solarniPaneli.description: MWT solar panels — 21.5% efficiency, 30-year warranty, no microcracks.
solarniPaneli.techDesc1: In conventional PERC cells, the front-side busbars shade up to 3% of the surface. MWT technology eliminates these busbars by routing current contacts through micro-holes to the back side of the cell.
solarniPaneli.techDesc2: The result: greater active area, higher efficiency, and significantly reduced risk of microcracks — the most common cause of degradation in traditional panels.
solarniPaneli.adv1Desc: 21.5% efficiency under standard conditions — up to 12% more energy than conventional PERC panels.
solarniPaneli.adv2Desc: IP68 protection rating, anodized aluminum frame, and corrosion resistance for 30+ years of operation.
solarniPaneli.adv3Desc: Only 0.4% annual degradation. After 30 years, panels still produce over 84% of their initial power.
solarniPaneli.adv4Desc: Optimal performance in cloudy weather, diffuse light, and high temperatures.
solarniPaneli.portfolioSubtitle: We offer panels from MWT to ABC technology — from reliable mid-range to record-breaking 24% efficiency.
solarniPaneli.3dHint: Rotate with your mouse to view from every angle
solarniPaneli.compNote: * HJT achieves higher laboratory efficiency, but at a significantly higher price. MWT offers the optimal balance between performance, durability, and price.
solarniPaneli.pullQuote: MWT technology eliminates microcracks — the most common cause of power loss in conventional panels.
solarniPaneli.panelFaq1Q: How many years do solar panels last?
solarniPaneli.panelFaq1A: MWT panels have a 30-year performance warranty. After 30 years, they produce a minimum of 84.3% of rated power.
solarniPaneli.panelFaq2A: Usually, rain is sufficient. For heavy soiling — wash with a soft hose and water. Avoid abrasive materials.
solarniPaneli.panelFaq3A: With SolarEdge P950 optimizers, each panel operates independently. Shading on one panel does not affect the others.
solarniPaneli.panelFaq4A: The warranty covers manufacturing defects (12 years) and performance (30 years). If output drops below guaranteed values, panels are replaced free of charge.

nasledstvo.heroDesc: Solaron's story begins two decades ago in the Netherlands. Today, we bring that expertise to Bulgaria — with the same commitment to quality.
nasledstvo.pullQuote1: Quality is not an accident — it's the result of thousands of correct decisions made on site.
nasledstvo.milestone1Desc: Adore started as a photovoltaic installation company in the Netherlands with a focus on quality and safety.

nasledstvo.milestone4Desc: Installation of one of the largest rooftop PV systems in Rotterdam — the Ahoy arena.
nasledstvo.milestone5Desc: Adore enters the Bulgarian market, bringing European standards for design, installation, and after-sales service.

nasledstvo.videoDesc: See how our team works on a large-scale industrial roof — from panel logistics to the final structure.
nasledstvo.pullQuote2: Experience from thousands of roofs in Europe gives us the confidence to deliver the same quality in Bulgaria.
nasledstvo.ctaTitle: Be part of the next chapter
nasledstvo.ctaSubtitle: Our story continues with every new project. Let yours be the next.

general.g1q: What is a grid-connected photovoltaic system?
general.g1a: It is an installation that produces electricity from sunlight and feeds it to the internal switchboard and/or to the distribution grid through an inverter.
general.g2q: How long do modern solar modules last?
general.g2a: Quality modules have a linear performance warranty of 25–30 years with expected annual degradation within a few percent.
general.g3q: Do I need a special roof for solar?
general.g3a: A 'special' roof is not required, but the supporting structure must be able to bear the load of the mounting system and modules.
general.g4q: Can I expand the system later?
general.g4a: Often yes, but it depends on the inverter, available input capacity, and grid conditions.
general.g5q: How do I choose a reliable installer?
general.g5a: Check references, warranty terms, detail of the quote, and clarity on timelines with the utility company.

install.i1q: How long does a typical residential system installation take?
install.i1a: The actual installation typically takes from a few days to two weeks depending on the scope, roof access, and weather.
install.i2q: Does the owner need to be present during installation?
install.i2a: It's recommended at least during key stages — agreeing on cable routes, inverter placement, and switchboard access.
install.i3q: What is the procedure for flat roofs?
install.i3a: Ballasted or fixed systems are used depending on the structural assessment and regulations.
install.i4a: It is the final switch-on after verifying insulation, polarity, protections, and inverter parameters.
install.i5q: How is the system maintained after installation?
install.i5a: Periodic inspection of cables and structure, cleaning when dust or leaves accumulate, and monitoring for alarms.

pricing.p1q: What determines the final installation price?
pricing.p1a: The capacity and grade of modules and inverter, roof complexity, cable route length, and whether a battery is included.
pricing.p2a: Potential extras include switchboard replacement, groundwork, structural reinforcement, or utility company fees.
pricing.p3q: How is the return on investment estimated?
pricing.p3a: By comparing the investment with expected annual savings on electricity bills.
pricing.p4q: Can I finance the purchase with installments?
pricing.p4a: Bank products and leasing are available from various institutions with varying terms.
pricing.p5q: Is adding a battery always economically justified?
pricing.p5a: Not automatically. The battery increases capital costs and has a limited cycle life.

technical.t1q: What is MPPT and why is it important?
technical.t1a: MPPT is the algorithm the inverter uses to keep the string near the maximum power point under changing conditions.
technical.t2q: What does PERC mean for modules?
technical.t2a: PERC is a technology for a passivated rear layer that reduces cell losses and increases efficiency.
technical.t3a: For complex roofs with multiple orientations, shading, or when you want module-level monitoring.
technical.t4a: Based on current, route length, allowable voltage drop, and installation temperature.
technical.t5q: What is inductive interference between cables?
technical.t5a: Alternating currents create fields; if cables are improperly arranged, interference can occur.

legal.l1q: Who is the connection agreement signed with?
legal.l1a: With the electricity distribution company (DSO) for the area where the property is located.
legal.l2q: Is a building permit needed for a rooftop structure?
legal.l2a: It depends on the type of intervention, structural elements, and local regulations.
legal.l3a: Typically, proof of the right to install on the building/property and access to the switchboard is expected.
legal.l4q: How is energy fed into the grid metered?
legal.l4a: Through metering devices and a contractual metering arrangement.
legal.l5q: What are the roles of EWRC and the RES Act for end customers?
legal.l5a: EWRC (Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) sets the regulatory framework for the sector; the RES Act (Renewable Energy Sources Act) is the key law governing renewables.

Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.heroSubtitle: Reduce operating costs by up to 70%. Accelerated tax depreciation. Professional energy audit.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.benefit1Desc: A solar system reduces electricity costs by up to 70%, with savings starting from the first month.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.benefit2Desc: Accelerated tax depreciation over 4 years. The solar system investment is deducted from the tax base.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.benefit3Desc: Demonstrate environmental commitment. Compliance with ESG standards and the CSRD directive.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.benefit4Desc: Commercial properties with solar systems are valued higher and attract higher-quality tenants.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.process1Desc: Detailed analysis of consumption, tariffs, and load profile.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.process2Desc: NPV, IRR, payback period, and scenarios at different electricity prices.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.process4Desc: Professional installation without disrupting business operations.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.ppaDesc: You only pay for the energy produced at a price lower than grid rates.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.leasingDesc: Monthly payments aligned with your cash flow.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq1Q: What is the minimum size of a business system?
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq1A: For business clients, we typically start from 10 kWp, which covers the needs of a mid-sized office or shop.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq2Q: Can I use accelerated tax depreciation?
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq2A: Yes. Solar systems can be depreciated over 4 years on an accelerated basis, significantly reducing the tax base.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq3Q: How does it affect ESG reporting?
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq3A: Installing a solar system directly reduces Scope 2 emissions and improves ESG metrics.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq4Q: Can installation be done without stopping business operations?
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq4A: Yes. We plan the installation so it doesn't disrupt normal business operations.
Solutions_ZaBiznesa_Extended.faq5A: At current electricity prices, the typical ROI for business systems is 3–5 years.

panels.title: High-Power Panels
panels.desc: Tier-1 bifacial modules 550–600 Wp with a 25-year performance warranty.

inverters.title: Industrial Inverters
inverters.desc: On-grid: SolarEdge, Kstar, Huawei, SMA — string and central inverters with 98.5%+ efficiency.

scada.title: SCADA Monitoring
scada.desc: 24/7 monitoring of every string with automatic alarms and remote diagnostics.

storage.title: Energy Storage
storage.desc: Kstar and CNTE cabinet-type energy storage systems.

compliance.title: Protection and Compliance
compliance.desc: Full compliance with BDS, IEC 62446, and utility company requirements.

caseStudy.description: Ground-mounted industrial power plant for a manufacturing facility in Saedinenie. Covers 85% of the factory's energy needs and achieves full payback in under 4 years.

Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq1Q: What is the minimum size of an industrial solar system?
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq1A: We typically start from 100 kWp for industrial projects, which covers the needs of a mid-sized manufacturing facility.
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq2Q: How long does it take to build a MW system?
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq2A: The typical timeframe is 3–6 months, including design, permits, and installation.
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq3A: We combine solar production with intelligent load management and, when needed, battery storage.
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq4Q: What permits are required for an industrial system?
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq4A: For systems over 30 kWp, a utility company assessment, detailed project, building permit, and connection agreement are required.
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq5Q: What is a realistic ROI for an industrial solar system?
Solutions_ZaIndustriyata_Extended.faq5A: At current electricity prices and with accelerated tax depreciation, the typical ROI is 2–4 years.

Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq1Q: What are the structural requirements for installation?
Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq1A: A level concrete or asphalt surface with a minimum bearing capacity of 2.5 kN/m² is required.
Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq2A: Charging stations are built directly into the structure's columns. Cabling runs inside the aluminum profiles.
Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq3A: With a combined model (parking + energy + EV charging), the typical ROI period is 5–7 years.
Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq4Q: Can the structure withstand strong winds?
Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq4A: The structure is designed for wind loads up to 160 km/h (Zone III per Eurocode).
Solutions_SolarenKarport_Extended.faq5A: For carports over 200 m², a building permit is required. We manage the entire process.

Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq1Q: How long do off-grid system batteries last?
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq1A: LiFePO₄ batteries have a lifespan of 6000+ cycles, which means 15–20 years with daily use.
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq2Q: How does the system work during winter?
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq2A: In winter, solar production is lower, but a properly sized system compensates with a larger battery array.
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq3Q: What maintenance does an off-grid system require?
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq3A: Minimal — cleaning the panels 2–3 times per year and checking battery connections.
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq4Q: Can I add a grid connection later?
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq4A: Yes. Hybrid inverters allow future grid connection without replacing equipment.
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq5Q: How is an off-grid system sized?
Solutions_AvtonomniSistemi_Extended.faq5A: We calculate daily consumption in kWh, multiply by the desired days of autonomy, and add 20% reserve.

whyBattery.reason3Desc: Reduce peak power and save on grid charges.
whyBattery.reason4Desc: Protection from future electricity price increases.

solarBoost.title: Solar Boost
solarBoost.desc: Automatically prioritizes charging from solar panels. When the sun is shining — you charge for free.

smartCharging.title: Smart Charging

compatibility.title: Universal Compatibility

installation.title: Easy Installation

photovoltaicEffect.title: Photovoltaic Effect
photovoltaicEffect.desc: Sunlight hits the solar cells and releases electrons, creating direct current (DC).

inverterConversion.title: Conversion
inverterConversion.desc: The inverter converts direct current into alternating current (AC), used by household appliances.

consumption.title: Consumption
consumption.desc: The generated energy powers your appliances. Excess goes to the grid or battery.

netMetering.title: Net Metering
netMetering.desc: Excess energy is fed back to the grid. You only pay the difference between produced and consumed.

vatZero.title: 0% VAT for households
vatZero.desc: Residential photovoltaic systems in Bulgaria are exempt from VAT — a 20% direct discount on the price.

taxAmortization.title: Accelerated tax depreciation
taxAmortization.desc: For business clients: solar systems can be depreciated on an accelerated basis over 4 years.

JsonLd.orgDescription: Design and installation of photovoltaic systems with 20+ years of European experience. SolarEdge, Kstar, Deye inverters and AIKO, DMEGC, Sunport Power panels.
JsonLd.homeSystemDesc: Design and installation of residential photovoltaic systems from 3 to 30 kWp with SolarEdge, Kstar, and Deye inverters.
JsonLd.businessSystemDesc: Industrial and commercial photovoltaic installations up to 1 MWp with monitoring and maintenance.
JsonLd.storageSystemDesc: Battery energy storage systems from Kstar, CNTE, and Pylontech for energy independence.
JsonLd.serviceDesc: Full engineering — from design and procurement to installation and commissioning of photovoltaic systems for homes, businesses, and industry.
JsonLd.faq1Q: How much does a solar system installation cost?
JsonLd.faq1A: The price depends on the system size and components. An average residential system of 5-10 kWp costs between €4,000 and €9,000. We offer a free consultation with a personalized quote.
JsonLd.faq2Q: How long does it take to recoup the investment?
JsonLd.faq2A: For a typical residential system, the payback period is 4-6 years, depending on consumption, roof orientation, and tariff plan.
JsonLd.faq3A: We offer a 30-year warranty on panels, 12-year on inverters, and 10-year on batteries. Installation comes with a 5-year warranty.
JsonLd.faq4Q: What inverter brands do you offer?
JsonLd.faq4A: We work with three leading brands: SolarEdge (premium), Kstar (hybrid all-in-one solutions), and Deye (hybrid inverters with excellent value for money). For commercial projects, we also offer Huawei.
JsonLd.faq5A: Net metering allows you to sell excess generated energy back to the grid. The meter records the difference between consumed and produced energy, which significantly reduces your electricity bills.
